question.text: Which of the following best describes the term 'fifth Beatle'?

options.text: A person who had a significant influence on the Beatles' career
A member of the Beatles who was fired
A nickname given to John Lennon's pet
A bandmate of the Beatles who left before fame

prediction.answer_text: A person who had a significant influence on the Beatles' career

metadata.retrieval_query: Which of the following best describes the term 'fifth Beatle'?
metadata.evidence_preview:
[1] Fifth Beatle - Wikipedia
URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fifth_Beatle
Fifth Beatle The fifth Beatle is an informal title that has been applied to people who were at one point a member of the Beatles or who had a strong association with John Lennon, Paul McCartney, George Harrison, and Ringo Starr. The term originated in 1964 with the American disc jockey Murray the K, who pronounced himself the "fifth Beatle" due to the amount of promotion and coverage he afforded the band on his radio programme

. The term "fifth Beatle" was first used a year later when they crossed the Atlantic in an attempt to crack America. options.text: Her first appearance at the Apollo Theater
Her solo performance at the Roseland Ballroom
Her performance with the Chick Webb Orchestra at Yale University
Taking over the Chick Webb Orchestra after Webb's death

prediction.answer_text: Her first appearance at the Apollo Theater

Ella Fitzgerald Ella Jane Fitzgerald (April 25, 1917 – June 15, 1996) was an American improvisational vocal musician, singer, songwriter and composer, sometimes referred to as the "First Lady of Song", "Queen of Jazz", and "Lady Ella".
